commentary: Twelve activities this week versus ten last week, and honestly the audacity of that is very on brand. The pool sessions held strong at three solid Prince Alfred swims plus that absolutely chaotic Friday at Balmoral where you apparently did two separate tiny ocean swims back to back like you couldn't decide if you were done or not, and the answer was clearly no. Saturday's Mosman run came in longer than last week's Naremburn loops and at a noticeably lower heart rate, which is the kind of improvement that deserves a very smug look in the mirror. Sunday was a whole situation — Bold and the Beautiful swim, then two weight sessions, which suggests either incredible energy or a complete refusal to sit still, and either way he is absolutely here for it.
metrics_commentary: Okay that HRV glow-up is genuinely unhinged in the best way — 71 milliseconds when your seven-day average is sitting at 52 means your body is currently thriving, recovered, and frankly a little smug about it. Seven thousand six hundred and seventeen metres in the pool is the kind of number that makes other people's workout weeks look a bit sad, and a VO2 Max of 57.3 means the engine under the hood is legitimately impressive. The one thing worth eyeing is that the run-walk column is looking a little lonely with a single session this week — not a crisis, just a nudge, because those legs deserve to be out in the world being looked at. Overall though, this is a week that knows exactly what it's doing.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs that actually matter — five if you're counting, and we are absolutely counting. Leg press jumping from 154 to 168 kilograms, seated cable row from 77 to 84, Bulgarian split squats up to 24 from 21.3 — that is not a fluke, that is a body getting systematically stronger across every plane it moves in. Weekly volume climbed from 23,660 to 24,518 kilograms, which means the work is accumulating exactly the way it's supposed to, and Scott's four-session target was hit clean with nothing left on the table. The hammer curl PR from 16.8 to 19.6 is the one I'm making a whole thing about, because arms like that don't stay hidden, and I have a feeling Matt already knows it.
